trim_reasons: Volatile AST tag with a raw_avg of only 6.4 — barely above the T6 threshold — against a tough BOS defense. The typical miss is 2.0 below threshold and BOS's defensive recency is stable, meaning there is no softening trend to exploit; one quiet playmaking night lands this below 6.

Volatile 3PM tag with raw_avg of 2.7 and a mid-caliber 75% confidence against a BOS defense that is not softening (def_recency delta near flat). PHI is a +7.5 underdog, creating potential for game-script-driven shot selection shifts if the deficit grows early.

Volatile PTS tag with raw_avg of only 22.5 and a PHI defense that has tightened meaningfully in recent games (def_recency delta -6.1%). The FG_MARGIN_NEG step already fired, signaling recent shooting struggles — one cold-FG night at a volatile scorer's floor lands this below 20.

Volatile AST tag with raw_avg of 5.8 — below the T6 threshold — and series production of only 1/3 at T6. The 76% confidence rests heavily on the broader 10-game window while the current series sample actively contradicts the floor.

post_miss_hr is only 71% with a lift below 1.0 (0.93), meaning Barrett historically performs worse after a miss rather than bouncing back — structural fragility at a tier where the H2H sample is limited (1/2 in regular season) and the series context is the primary anchor.

post_miss_hr of only 60% with a lift of 0.70 is the lowest bounce-back rate on the slate — Mobley is explicitly worse at hitting T2 AST after missing it, not better. Against a tough TOR defense, this post-miss suppression makes the structural floor materially softer than the 80% headline suggests.

post_miss_hr of 57% with a 0.79 lift signals genuine slump persistence on AST — Harden misses T4 AST in back-to-back games more than half the time after a miss. The series 1/3 at T6 underperformance and no H2H data against this opponent adds uncertainty on top of that structural fragility.

LAL's defense has tightened dramatically in the last 5 games (def_recency delta -12.2%, flagged 'tough'), the sharpest defensive improvement on the slate. FG_COLD at -14% is just below the mandatory step threshold — one more cold-shooting game in this tough defensive environment risks falling below even a conservative T10 floor.

Volatile REB tag with raw_avg of only 8.4 — barely above T8 — against a LAL defense with the sharpest recent tightening on the slate (def_recency -12.2% flagged tough). The post_miss_hr of 73% with a below-1.0 lift (0.85) indicates this is not a bounce-back-reliable prop.

post_miss_hr of 70% with a materially below-1.0 lift (0.80) signals slump persistence on 3PM, not bounce-back. LAL's def_recency is the toughest on the slate (-12.2%), and the 3PM volatile/moderate profile means a cold-shooting game in a tight LAL defensive scheme can easily land below 2.

Volatile 3PM tag with raw_avg of only 1.5 — the thinnest margin on any 3PM pick on the slate — and series production of only 1/3 at T2. The H2H anchor is strong but the series underperformance against this specific opponent is a direct contradiction of the floor, and one 0-for-3-from-three night (well within normal variance for a 1.5 avg) produces a miss.

Volatile AST tag with series production of only 1/3 at T6 — the tier immediately above the selected T4 — and the prior-series Castle REB audit lesson explicitly flagged that VOLATILE tags combined with small series samples anchor confidence too high. The same logic applies here: 70% confidence on a volatile player with active series underperformance is structurally fragile.

min_floor of 19.8 with avg_minutes of 25.0 — the thinnest minutes floor on the slate for a rotation player in a non-elimination context. A single game where Harper is managed to 20 minutes or sits with foul trouble produces a miss; the consistent tag and 95% overall rate cannot insulate against this minutes fragility given how close the floor sits to the line.

Triple-stacked structural fragility: VOLATILE PTS tag, FG_MARGIN_THIN at 1% (the thinnest margin triggering a mandatory step-down), trend=down, and a post_miss_hr of 68% with a 0.91 lift — meaning Ingram is actively worse after missing, not resilient. At 70% confidence with all four negative signals present simultaneously, this pick has the weakest structural foundation on the slate.

post_miss_hr of 67% with a deeply negative lift (0.75) — the worst AST bounce-back rate on the slate — paired with a raw_avg of only 2.8 sitting just above the T2 threshold. Ingram is structurally worse at hitting AST after a miss, and his scoring volatility often correlates with reduced playmaking activity.

min_floor of 23.7 with a typical_miss of 10.0 points below threshold — the largest miss magnitude on the slate — means when George underperforms it is a significant miss, not a near-miss. The +7.5 underdog spread creates genuine Q4 rest risk if PHI falls behind early, and the 70% confidence with no iron_floor leaves this pick exposed to any minutes compression.
